item.title: Famitsu Sales: 3/16/26 – 3/22/26
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Pokemon Pokopia remains on top while Crimson Desert opens at 31,108 retail sales.

Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of March 16, 2026 to March 22, 2026.

Pokemon Pokopia for Switch 2 remained the highest selling game of the week following its debut the previous week, moving an additional 114,515 retail copies.

Crimson Desert for PlayStation 5 was the highest selling new release, opening at 31,108 retail copies sold.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 81,518 units, the Switch family sold 18,754 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 14,119 units, the Xbox Series family sold 209 units, and the PlayStation 4 family sold 19 units.

Get the full sales charts below.

Software Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. [SW2] Pokemon Pokopia (The Pokemon Company, 03/05/26) – 114,515 (7,702,915)
  2. [PS5] Crimson Desert (Pearl Abyss, 03/19/26) – 31,108 (New)
  3. [SW2] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) – 10,294 (48,012)
  4. [SW2] Mario Kart World (Nintendo, 06/05/25) – 8,276 (2,873,338)
  5. [SW2] Mario Tennis Fever (Nintendo, 02/12/26) – 7,833 (90,975)
  6. [PS5] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) – 6,652 (198,271)
  7. [NSW] Pokemon FireRed Version / LeafGreen Version (Download Card) (The Pokemon Company, 02/28/26) – 5,872 (5,932)
  8. [NSW] Animal Crossing: New Horizons (Nintendo, 03/20/20) – 4,760 (8,407,095)
  9. [NSW] Minecraft (Microsoft, 06/21/18) – 4,451 (4,173,164)
  10. [SW2] Animal Crossing: New Horizons – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 01/15/26) – 3,784 (91,914)

Rankings 11 to 30 will be announced on March 27.

  1. [PS5] RUSHING BEAT X: Return of Brawl Brothers (City Connection, 03/19/26) – (New)
  2. [SW2] RUSHING BEAT X: Return of Brawl Brothers (City Connection, 03/19/26) – (New)
  3. [SW2] Bang! Bang! Bandits – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nippon Columbia, 03/19/26) – (New)
  4. [NSW] Virtual Ties: Isekaijoucho Traumerei (Entergram, 03/19/26) – (New)
  5. [PS5] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) –
  6. [PS5] FATAL FRAME II: Crimson Butterfly REMAKE (Koei Tecmo, 03/12/26) –
  7. [SW2] FATAL FRAME II: Crimson Butterfly REMAKE (Koei Tecmo, 03/12/26) –
  8. [NSW] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  9. [SW2] Pokemon Legends: Z-A – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  10. [SW2] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  11. [SW2] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  12. [SW2] Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV (Nintendo, 07/24/25) –
  13. [NSW] Pokemon Legends: Z-A (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  14. [SW2] Kirby Air Riders (Nintendo, 11/20/25) –
  15. [SW2] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  16. [NSW] Super Mario Party Jamboree (Nintendo, 10/17/24) –
  17. [NSW] Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Nintendo, 12/07/18) –
  18. [NSW] Mario Kart 8 Deluxe (Nintendo, 04/28/17) –
  19. [PS5] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  20. [NSW] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  21. [NSW] Nintendo Switch Sports (Nintendo, 04/29/22) –
  22. [PS5] Tokyo Xtreme Racer (Genki, 02/26/26) –
  23. [SW2] Donkey Kong Bananza (Nintendo, 07/17/25) –
  24. [NSW] R-Type Tactics I • II Cosmos (Granzella, 03/12/26) –
  25. [NSW] Splatoon 3 (Nintendo, 09/09/22) –
  26. [NSW] Clubhouse Games: 51 Worldwide Classics (Nintendo, 06/05/20) –

Hardware Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. Switch 2 – 81,518 (4,885,791)
  2. PlayStation 5 Digital Edition – 8,658 (1,224,433)
  3. Switch OLED Model – 8,262 (951,139)
  4. Switch – 5,873 (20,262,015)
  5. Switch Lite – 4,619 (6,894,300)
  6. PlayStation 5 – 2,930 (5,907,115)
  7. PlayStation 5 Pro – 2,531 (336,046)
  8. Xbox Series X Digital Edition – 89 (28,731)
  9. Xbox Series S – 62 (341,432)
  10. Xbox Series X – 58 (325,401)
  11. PlayStation 4 – 19 (7,930,410)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/famitsu-sales-3-16-26-3-22-26
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Side-scrolling shoot ’em up ZPF launches April 16 for Xbox Series, Xbox One, Switch, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

First released for Genesis in 2025.

Side-scrolling shoot ’em up game ZPF, which first launched for Genesis / Mega Drive on November 20, 2025 via the Mega Cat Studios Store, will come to Xbox Series, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam on April 16 for $9.99, publisher Mega Cat Studios and developer ZPF Team announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Mega Cat Studios:

About

ZPF is a retro-inspired horizontal shooter for the SEGA Genesis / Mega Drive. Blast your way through a cosmic gauntlet of epic boss battles, addictive scoring, and hidden secrets in a thrilling arcade-style adventure through fantasy, science-fiction, and alien-themed worlds.

Inspired by classic horizontal shooters like Lords of Thunder, Elemental Master, and the Thunderforce series, ZPF is a passion project from the designer and developer of Super XYX and Tanzer.

Key Features

  • Over Six Stages of Full-On Pixel Mayhem – Players will dodge and weave through intense bullet patterns, unleashing a barrage of firepower upon hordes of unique and challenging enemies.
  • Three Playable Characters – Choose your hero—from the teleporting trickster to the laser-wielding warrior! Each character boasts a distinct playstyle with unique shots and melee attacks.
  • Boss Battles – Brace yourself for epic encounters with mid and end-stage bosses, each demanding a different strategy to defeat.
  • Shop Buffs and Hints – Upgrade your arsenal and gain valuable tips between levels at each of the world’s in-game shops.
  • Secrets Galore – Finish every stage and discover a treasure trove of hidden secrets.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/side-scrolling-shoot-em-up-zpf-launches-april-16-for-xbox-series-xbox-one-switch-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mullet MadJack coming to Switch on April 30
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Currently available for Xbox Series, Xbox One, and PC.

Hammer95 will release anime-inspired fast-paced first-person shooter Mullet MadJack for Switch on April 30, the developer announced.

Mullet MadJack first launched for PC via Steam and GOG on May 15, 2024, followed by Xbox Series and Xbox One on March 13, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via Hammer95:

About

Developed by Hammer95 Studios and published by Epopeia Games, Mullet MadJack has won over hundreds of thousands of players around the world with its unique premise: a first-person shooter where you must eliminate enemies every 10 seconds to survive in a future dominated by billionaire robots—all wrapped in a visual style that pays homage to classic anime from the ’80s and ’90s.

The Nintendo Switch release follows a successful run on other platforms. The game quickly became a phenomenon on Steam, amassing around 97% positive reviews and hundreds of thousands of players, as well as ranking among the best-selling titles in its genre on the platform. On Xbox, the title further expanded its player base, surpassing 800,000 downloads across Steam and Game Pass.

Recognition also came from critics and the industry. Mullet MadJack won the Best Brazilian Game award at the gamescom latam BIG Festival 2025, establishing itself as one of the leading highlights of the national and international indie scene.

On the Nintendo Switch, the game retains all the intensity that made it a hit: fast-paced combat, roguelite-style progression, dozens of power-ups, and an insane vertical campaign where every floor is a new challenge—and every second counts.

The launch of April 30 will happen during gamescom latam 2026 and it marks a new chapter for the title, now accessible to a new audience and ready to captivate the players in the Nintendo ecosystem as well.

Key Features

  • Exciting and Dynamic Combat – Race against time and infiltrate a building alone, overcoming each floor until you reach your objective.
  • Campaign Mode – Clear floors filled with robots, and in crucial moments, execute ultraviolent finishes on your enemies to survive.
  • Survival Mode – Utilize your skills in an endless mode and outdo your friends.
  • Unique Visual and Art Style – The contrasting colors between black and vibrant neon evoke the aesthetics of violent and adult anime from the era of VHS tapes.
  • Engaging Narrative – Set in an Old School cyberpunk world full of robots, the aesthetics of 90s anime merge with an action-packed environment.
  • Nostalgic Soundtrack – Inspired by the synthwave genre, the soundtrack sets the tone and urban atmosphere of the game.
  • Evolve Your Character – Enhance your character with smart choices so that you can reach the top floor in the best possible time or you’ll have to try again, all in the best Rogue-Lite style. But remember that each upgrade will affect how the game is played.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/mullet-madjack-coming-to-switch-on-april-30
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
